message:
chore: close completed beads (caam-vpu2, caam-tc6p, caam-6sao.4)

- Close caam-vpu2: WezTerm recover command state machine complete
- Close caam-tc6p: State machine unit tests comprehensive
- Close caam-6sao.4: OAuth URL extraction tests with multiline fixtures
- Add extensive OAuth URL extraction tests (ANSI, trailing punctuation,
  query param preservation)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -97,7 +97,7 @@
 {"id":"caam-6sao.1","title":"Task: WezTerm recovery structured logging + trace IDs","description":"Goal
- Add structured logging to the WezTerm recovery flow so pane automation is auditable and debuggable.

Details
- Log per-pane state transitions, detection matches, injections, and errors with fields: pane_id, window_id, tab_title (if available), tool, state, action, reason, timestamp.
- Emit a correlation/run ID to group events across panes.
- Support verbosity flags (info/debug) and a JSON log mode.
- Redact OAuth URLs and codes by default; only print in explicit report output.

Success
- A failed recovery can be diagnosed from logs alone without re-running the flow.","notes":"Depends on: caam-sssb (pane discovery) and caam-vpu2 (state machine).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:46:57.837047380Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:01:30.244662840Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.1","depends_on_id":"caam-6sao","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:46:57.839060311Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.1","depends_on_id":"caam-sssb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:47:01.584581662Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.1","depends_on_id":"caam-vpu2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:47:06.798771702Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-6sao.2","title":"Task: WezTerm recovery E2E script + fixtures","description":"Goal
- Provide a repeatable end-to-end smoke test for WezTerm recovery using fixtures and a mocked wezterm CLI.

Details
- Build fixture scenarios: single pane, multi-pane, mixed providers, rate-limit + login selection + URL extraction + code injection.
- Include a compaction banner scenario to validate reminder injection when enabled.
- Capture expected send-text calls and verify order/timing.
- Emit verbose, redacted logs per scenario with timestamps, pane ids, and matched patterns.
- Document a one-command run for CI or local validation.

Success
- A single command can validate the recovery flow without a live WezTerm mux.","notes":"Depends on: caam-kbif (mock CLI harness) and caam-vpu2 (recovery state machine).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:47:14.270225464Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:03:31.160722267Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.2","depends_on_id":"caam-6sao","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:47:14.271731891Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.2","depends_on_id":"caam-6sao.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:04:40.022616496Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.2","depends_on_id":"caam-kbif","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:47:18.307384892Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.2","depends_on_id":"caam-vpu2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:47:22.168541814Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-6sao.3","title":"Task: Pane discovery unit tests + matcher fixtures","description":"Goal

- Assert no false positives on generic shells or other CLIs.

- Gemini tool markers (3 variants)
- Non-matching panes (11 common CLI outputs)
- ANSI/box-drawing fixtures (5 variants)
- Override (--match) fixtures (5 variants)
- Cross-tool fixtures (4 variants)

Tests:
- TestPaneDiscoveryFixtures: 42 fixture-based tests
- TestNormalizeWeztermTextFixtures: 12 ANSI/box-drawing tests
- TestMatchWeztermPaneRateLimitVariants: 16 rate limit pattern tests
- TestMatchWeztermPaneToolMarkerVariants: 17 tool marker tests
- TestNoFalsePositivesOnCommonCLIs: 10 false positive prevention tests
- TestStripBoxDrawingComprehensive: 8 box-drawing removal tests

All 91 tests emit structured logs: fixture name, tool, matched, reason, expected values.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.3","depends_on_id":"caam-6sao","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:59:29.541102042Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.3","depends_on_id":"caam-kbif","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:59:36.547532959Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.3","depends_on_id":"caam-sssb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:59:33.771976303Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"caam-6sao.5","title":"Task: login-all command tests + safety prompts","description":"Goal
- Validate caam wezterm login-all behavior across dry-run, confirmation, and selection modes.

Details
- Test --all, --match, --subscription, --yes, and --dry-run behaviors.
- Ensure no injections occur on non-matching panes unless --all is explicit.
- Verify prompt text and summary output include counts of targeted/skipped/failed panes.
- Emit structured logs in tests for pane id, action, and outcome.

Success
- Batch login is safe, predictable, and well-instrumented.","notes":"Depends on: caam-fiw4 (login-all command) and caam-kbif (mock CLI harness).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:59:52.217782146Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T17:59:57.914626184Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.5","depends_on_id":"caam-6sao","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:59:52.219568831Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.5","depends_on_id":"caam-fiw4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:00:03.607901572Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.5","depends_on_id":"caam-kbif","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:00:07.489826956Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-6sao.6","title":"Task: Competitive research (OpenCode login handling)","description":"Goal
- Extract actionable patterns from OpenCode’s login handling to improve caam’s WezTerm recovery flow.

Details
- Review OpenCode’s auth/login recovery implementation and any terminal automation patterns.
- Identify reusable ideas: detection heuristics, prompt handling, state machines, or UX flows.
- Document findings and map them to caam changes (what to adopt, what to avoid).
- If any code changes are proposed, specify which existing beads they update and ensure tests are included.

Tests
- If integration changes are made, update or add unit + E2E tests in the relevant recovery beads.

Success
- We either confidently confirm caam’s current approach or incorporate proven patterns, with rationale documented.

 {"id":"caam-6v8","title":"Test sync/machine.go & identity.go","description":"# Test Machine & Identity Operations

## Files: internal/sync/machine.go, internal/sync/identity.go

## Functions to Test (machine.go)
- Machine struct creation and validation
- Machine status updates
- Machine connectivity checks

## Functions to Test (identity.go)
- GetOrCreateLocalIdentity
- Local machine ID generation
- Identity persistence

## Test Cases Required

### Machine Tests
1. **TestMachine_NewMachine** - Creates valid machine
2. **TestMachine_Validate** - Validates required fields
3. **TestMachine_UpdateStatus** - Updates online/offline
4. **TestMachine_SetLastSeen** - Updates timestamp

### Identity Tests
1. **TestGetOrCreateLocalIdentity_New** - Creates new identity
2. **TestGetOrCreateLocalIdentity_Existing** - Returns existing
3. **TestLocalIdentity_Persistence** - Saved to disk
4. **TestLocalIdentity_UniqueID** - ID is unique per machine
5. **TestLocalIdentity_Hostname** - Captures hostname

## Implementation Notes
- Use t.TempDir() for persistence tests
- Test ID uniqueness across runs
- Use testutil.Harness for logging","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:03:24.068691448Z","updated_at":"2025-12-20T00:18:21.646607293Z","closed_at":"2025-12-20T00:18:21.646607293Z","close_reason":"Consolidated into package-level test bead for sync/","compaction_level":0}
@@ -414,7 +414,7 @@
 {"id":"caam-t5p","title":"Profile workspaces: Switch all tools at once","description":"## Problem
Switching contexts (home → work) requires multiple commands:
```bash
caam activate claude work-claude
caam activate codex work-codex  
caam activate gemini work-gemini
```

## Solution
Workspace presets that switch all tools atomically.

```bash
# Define workspaces
caam workspace create work \
  --claude=work-claude \
  --codex=work-codex \
  --gemini=work-gemini

caam workspace create home \
  --claude=personal \
  --codex=personal

# Switch everything at once
caam workspace work
# ✓ Switched to workspace 'work'
#   claude: work-claude
#   codex: work-codex
#   gemini: work-gemini
```

## Features
- Create named workspaces with profile mappings
- Switch all tools with one command
- Show current workspace in status
- Optional: Auto-detect workspace from directory (like direnv)

## Storage
```yaml
workspaces:
  work:
    claude: work-claude
    codex: work-codex
    gemini: work-gemini
  home:
    claude: personal
    codex: home
  
current: work
```

## Integration with Projects
Could also tie to project associations:
```bash
caam project set ~/code/work-repo --workspace=work
# Entering ~/code/work-repo auto-switches to work workspace
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:01:14.880968303Z","updated_at":"2025-12-20T02:36:04.750089226Z","closed_at":"2025-12-20T02:36:04.750089226Z","close_reason":"Implemented workspace command for switching all tools at once","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-t5p","depends_on_id":"caam-m9g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:03:33.451832994Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-ta7","title":"Implement provider list panel (left)","description":"## Task
Create the left panel showing the list of providers (Codex, Claude, Gemini).

## Design
- Vertical list of providers
- Currently selected provider highlighted
- Show count of profiles per provider
- Tab key cycles through providers

## Implementation
- Use bubbles/list or custom component
- Style with lipgloss (border, colors)
- Connect to main model's activeProvider

## Visual Example
```
┌─ Providers ──┐
│ ▶ Codex (3)  │
│   Claude (5) │
│   Gemini (2) │
└──────────────┘
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Shows all three providers
- Tab key cycles selection
- Profile counts accurate
- Visual highlight on selected","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-17T05:51:48.642239884Z","updated_at":"2025-12-17T06:32:43.562092985Z","closed_at":"2025-12-17T06:32:43.562092985Z","close_reason":"Implemented provider list panel with vertical layout, profile counts, selection highlighting, and integrated into main TUI layout. Added tests.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-ta7","depends_on_id":"caam-by4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-17T05:51:48.656836326Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-ta7","depends_on_id":"caam-qpx","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T05:52:05.677440151Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-tbwx","title":"Task: Local auth agent (Playwright + Chrome)","description":"Goal
- Implement a local Mac agent that completes browser OAuth flows and returns challenge codes to the remote coordinator.

Details
- Launch Chrome/Canary via Playwright with existing profile to reuse logged-in Google accounts.
- Select account using LRU strategy or explicit account list; update LRU after success.
- Extract challenge code and POST back to coordinator with request id.
- Handle retries, timeouts, and UI changes with selector fallbacks.
- Detect Playwright/Chrome availability and provide a guided install path when missing.
- Avoid logging codes or URLs except in explicit debug mode.

Tests
- Unit tests for LRU selection, retry logic, and response parsing.
- Integration tests with Playwright in headless mode using mocked pages.
- E2E test that drives a full login flow against a local fixture server.
- Structured logs with request_id, account_hint, and step timing (redacted).

Success
- Most rate-limit recoveries complete without manual copy/paste.
","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T16:28:35.671838028Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-22T04:25:43.455735717Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-tbwx","depends_on_id":"caam-3ezz","type":"discovered-from","created_at":"2026-01-16T16:28:35.693560843Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-tbwx","depends_on_id":"caam-3ezz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T16:31:37.397422743Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-tbwx","depends_on_id":"caam-ldxo.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:56:19.756708668Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"caam-telz","title":"Authwatch rejects profiles missing required backups","description":"Ensure authwatch getProfileHash errors when required auth backups are missing (unless AllowOptionalOnly applies); add tests covering required/optional cases.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-13T04:09:17.255941870Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-13T04:10:58.985209541Z","closed_at":"2026-01-13T04:10:58.985209541Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"caam-tfzr","title":"Epic: Remove broken Claude auth/session assumptions","description":"Purpose
- Identify and remove (or hard-disable) CAAM features that rely on incorrect assumptions about how Claude Code stores session/auth data, to avoid misleading users.

Background
- Recent Claude Code auth files no longer include email/accountId in ~/.claude/.credentials.json, and tokens appear opaque (non-JWT). Some features were built assuming those fields existed or could be decoded.
- Misleading features create user confusion and wasted time.

Scope
- Audit all Claude-specific auth/session parsing, status, health, refresh, watcher, and docs.
- Remove or hard-disable any feature that cannot be made correct without new data sources.
- Update CLI/TUI to avoid advertising unsupported behavior.
- Ensure any removals have migration notes or warnings for users.

Tests
- Epic-level test matrix: unit tests per affected module + E2E tests for Claude status output.
- Doc lint tests to ensure unsupported behaviors are not advertised.

Success Criteria
- No feature presents incorrect or unverifiable Claude auth/session information.
- Users are clearly guided to the supported workflows.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:20:07.104833103Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T02:18:40.205356301Z","compaction_level":0}
@@ -456,7 +456,7 @@
 {"id":"caam-v89","title":"Add --device-code flag to caam login command","description":"# Add --device-code flag to caam login (Profile Isolation Mode)

## IMPORTANT: Primary Workflow Doesn't Need This

For auth file swapping (the primary use case), users can already do:
\`\`\`bash
codex login --device-auth    # Native Codex command
caam backup codex account1   # Existing caam command
\`\`\`

No changes to caam needed for the primary workflow!

## When This Is Useful

This flag is for **Profile Isolation mode** - when users want isolated CODEX_HOME directories for parallel sessions:

\`\`\`bash
# Create isolated profiles with device-code auth
caam login codex work --device-code     # Isolated profile "work"
caam login codex personal --device-code # Isolated profile "personal"

# Run parallel sessions
caam exec codex work -- "task A"
caam exec codex personal -- "task B"   # Different account, simultaneous
\`\`\`

## Implementation (Simple)

Just pass \`--device-auth\` to the underlying \`codex login\` when flag is set:

\`\`\`go
if loginDeviceCode {
    if tool == "codex" {
        args = append(args, "--device-auth")
    } else {
        return fmt.Errorf("%s doesn't support --device-code\\n\\n"+
            "For headless setup, use native login + caam backup:\\n"+
            "  %s login\\n"+
            "  caam backup %s <name>", tool, tool, tool)
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Priority: P2

Useful for advanced Profile Isolation users, but primary workflow already works.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:38:13.433808220Z","updated_at":"2025-12-18T01:51:42.676486082Z","closed_at":"2025-12-18T01:51:42.676486082Z","close_reason":"Added --device-code flag to caam login; uses provider DeviceCodeProvider to run device-auth flow for codex isolated profiles.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-v89","depends_on_id":"caam-hzu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T22:40:07.882564117Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":28,"issue_id":"caam-v89","author":"ubuntu","text":"Starting: add --device-code to `caam login` (isolated profiles) and route to provider.DeviceCodeProvider when supported (Codex => codex login --device-auth). Will add tests + update codex provider SupportedAuthModes.","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:48:29Z"},{"id":30,"issue_id":"caam-v89","author":"ubuntu","text":"Landed in ff48b6d: `caam login codex <profile> --device-code` calls DeviceCodeProvider.LoginWithDeviceCode; errors if provider does not support device code. Tests updated for login flag presence.","created_at":"2025-12-18T01:51:52Z"}]}
 {"id":"caam-vek1","title":"Implement JWT parser for Codex auth.json","description":"# Task: Implement JWT parser for Codex auth.json

## Overview
Create a JWT parser that extracts identity information (email, organization, plan type) from the JWT ID token stored in Codex auth.json files WITHOUT making API calls.

## Background
OAuth tokens contain a JWT ID token with three parts: header.payload.signature (base64 encoded). The payload contains "claims" including:
- email: The account email
- plan_type or subscription_type: "max", "pro", "team", "enterprise"
- org or organization: Organization name (team accounts)
- exp: Expiration timestamp

We can base64-decode the payload WITHOUT verifying the signature (we trust it since it came from the provider).

## Technical Details

### Package: internal/identity/

```go
type Identity struct {
    Email        string    `json:"email,omitempty"`
    Organization string    `json:"organization,omitempty"`
    PlanType     string    `json:"plan_type,omitempty"`
    AccountID    string    `json:"account_id,omitempty"`
    ExpiresAt    time.Time `json:"expires_at,omitempty"`
    Provider     string    `json:"provider"`
}

// ExtractFromJWT parses a JWT token and extracts identity claims
func ExtractFromJWT(token string) (*Identity, error) {
    // 1. Split token by "."
    // 2. Base64-decode the payload (middle part)
    // 3. JSON unmarshal into claims struct
    // 4. Map claims to Identity struct
}

// ExtractFromCodexAuth reads Codex auth.json and extracts identity
func ExtractFromCodexAuth(path string) (*Identity, error) {
    // 1. Read auth.json
    // 2. Find id_token field
    // 3. Call ExtractFromJWT
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Correctly parses valid JWT tokens
- [ ] Extracts email from 100% of valid tokens
- [ ] Extracts plan_type when present
- [ ] Handles malformed tokens gracefully (returns error, not panic)
- [ ] Sub-millisecond extraction time (no network calls)
- [ ] Works offline

## Test Cases
1. Parse valid JWT with all fields
2. Parse JWT with minimal fields (email only)
3. Handle expired JWT (still extract identity)
4. Handle malformed JWT (invalid base64)
5. Handle JWT with unknown claims (ignore extra fields)

## Files to Create
- internal/identity/identity.go
- internal/identity/jwt.go
- internal/identity/codex.go
- internal/identity/identity_test.go

 {"id":"caam-vog","title":"caam verify: Validate all profile tokens","description":"## Problem
Profiles may have expired tokens or invalid auth without user knowing.

## Solution
Verification command that tests all profiles.

```bash
caam verify

Checking profile health...

Claude:
  ✓ work        Token valid (expires in 23h)
  ✓ personal    Token valid (expires in 4d)
  ✗ old-backup  Token expired (2 weeks ago)

Codex:
  ✓ dev         Token valid (expires in 6h) 
  ⚠ staging     Token expiring soon (45 min)

Gemini:
  ✓ main        Token valid (expires in 12h)

Summary: 5 healthy, 1 expiring soon, 1 expired

Recommendations:
  - Run 'caam refresh codex staging' to refresh expiring token
  - Delete or re-login to 'claude/old-backup'
```

## Features
1. Check token expiry for all profiles
2. Optionally verify with API call (--deep)
3. Show actionable recommendations
4. Return non-zero if issues found (for CI)

## Flags
```bash
caam verify              # Quick check (parse files only)
caam verify --deep       # API validation (slower)
caam verify --fix        # Auto-refresh expiring tokens
caam verify --json       # Machine-readable output
caam verify claude       # Check specific tool only
```

## Integration
- Could run automatically in `caam status`
- Daemon could run periodically
- CI/CD could verify before deployment","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:02:33.203251120Z","updated_at":"2025-12-20T02:49:10.846649627Z","closed_at":"2025-12-20T02:49:10.846649627Z","close_reason":"Implemented caam verify command with --json support, health calculation, and recommendations. 8 tests included.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-vog","depends_on_id":"caam-m9g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:03:58.987006271Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"caam-vpu2","title":"Feature: WezTerm recover command (state machine)","description":"Goal
- Automate the full per-pane recovery flow after rate limit: /login -> select subscription -> detect URL -> wait for code -> inject code -> resume prompt.

State Machine (per pane)
- IDLE: normal output
- RATE_LIMITED: detect rate-limit banner
- LOGIN_SENT: inject /login
- METHOD_SELECT: detect login method prompt -> inject 1
- URL_READY: extract OAuth URL
- CODE_WAIT: prompt operator / await local agent response
- CODE_SENT: inject code + Enter
- RESUME: detect login success -> inject resume prompt

UX + Safety
- Show a table of panes with state, match reason, and URL availability.
- Default to manual confirmation; never act on non-matching panes unless --all is explicit.
- Per-pane cooldown to avoid repeated injections when output is noisy.
- Manual override per pane (skip/force/retry).

Tests
- Unit tests for state machine transitions (see caam-tc6p).
- E2E tests with mocked wezterm CLI (see caam-6sao.2).
- Structured logs with pane_id, state, action, and errors.

Success
- Operator can recover 5-10 panes in one loop with minimal typing and clear feedback.

 {"id":"caam-w5op","title":"CLI: Add warning when all profiles for provider are in cooldown","description":"Show warning in status when ALL profiles for a provider are in cooldown.

SCENARIO: User has 3 Claude profiles, all hit rate limits. Status should warn:
  claude: ⚠️ ALL profiles in cooldown (next available in 23m)

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. After processing each provider, check if any profile is NOT in cooldown
2. If all are in cooldown:
   - Find the one with shortest remaining cooldown
   - Show warning with time until next available
3. Add to warnings list for display at end

This is critical UX - user needs to know they can't use ANY profile for this provider.

FILES: cmd/caam/cmd/root.go","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-20T01:26:01.525844528Z","updated_at":"2025-12-20T01:52:00.667597763Z","closed_at":"2025-12-20T01:52:00.667597763Z","close_reason":"Implemented all-profiles cooldown warning: shows when all profiles for a provider are in cooldown with next-available time","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-w5op","depends_on_id":"caam-gy7z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-20T01:27:27.607528387Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+// =============================================================================
+// OAuth URL Extraction Unit Tests - Multiline Fixtures (caam-6sao.4)
+// =============================================================================
+
+func TestExtractOAuthURL_MultilineFixtures(t *testing.T) {
+	tests := []struct {
+		name     string
+		output   string
+		expected string
+		desc     string
+	}{
+		{
+			name: "URL on single line",
+			output: `Please visit the following URL to complete authentication:
+https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?code_challenge=abc123&code_challenge_method=S256&client_id=claude-code&redirect_uri=http%3A%2F%2Flocalhost%3A12345%2Fcallback&response_type=code&scope=openid%20profile%20email
+Waiting for authentication...`,
+			expected: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?code_challenge=abc123&code_challenge_method=S256&client_id=claude-code&redirect_uri=http%3A%2F%2Flocalhost%3A12345%2Fcallback&response_type=code&scope=openid%20profile%20email",
+			desc:     "Full URL with multiple query params should be extracted completely",
+		},
+		{
+			name: "URL with complex query params",
+			output: `Open this URL: https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?code_challenge=xYz_ABC-123&state=abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz012345&nonce=1234567890abcdef`,
+			expected: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?code_challenge=xYz_ABC-123&state=abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz012345&nonce=1234567890abcdef",
+			desc:     "URL with special characters in query params",
+		},
+		{
+			name: "URL with URL-encoded values",
+			output: `Auth URL: https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?redirect_uri=http%3A%2F%2Flocalhost%3A8080%2Fauth%2Fcallback&scope=openid+profile+email`,
+			expected: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?redirect_uri=http%3A%2F%2Flocalhost%3A8080%2Fauth%2Fcallback&scope=openid+profile+email",
+			desc:     "URL with percent-encoded values preserved",
+		},
+		{
+			name:     "URL followed by punctuation",
+			output:   "Visit https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?code=abc. Then paste the code here.",
+			expected: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?code=abc.",
+			desc:     "URL followed by period - regex captures trailing dot (cleanURL would strip it)",
+		},
+		{
+			name:     "URL in parentheses",
+			output:   "(Open https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?token=xyz in browser)",
+			expected: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?token=xyz",
+			desc:     "URL within parentheses - should not capture closing paren",
+		},
+		{
+			name:     "URL in angle brackets",
+			output:   "Link: <https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?id=123>",
+			expected: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?id=123>",
+			desc:     "URL in angle brackets - regex may capture trailing bracket",
+		},
+		{
+			name: "Multiple URLs in output",
+			output: `Primary: https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?code=primary
+Alternative: https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?code=alternate`,
+			expected: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?code=primary",
+			desc:     "Multiple URLs - should extract first one (FindString behavior)",
+		},
+		{
+			name:     "URL with no query params",
+			output:   "Go to https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?",
+			expected: "",
+			desc:     "URL with empty query string - regex requires at least one char after ?",
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tt := range tests {
+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			url := ExtractOAuthURL(tt.output)
+			if url != tt.expected {
+				t.Errorf("ExtractOAuthURL() = %q, want %q\nDescription: %s", url, tt.expected, tt.desc)
+			}
+			// Log extraction success for structured test output
+			t.Logf("fixture=%s extraction_success=%v url_length=%d", tt.name, url == tt.expected, len(url))
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+func TestExtractOAuthURL_ANSIDecorated(t *testing.T) {
+	tests := []struct {
+		name     string
+		output   string
+		expected string
+		desc     string
+	}{
+		{
+			name:     "URL with bold ANSI",
+			output:   "\x1b[1mhttps://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?bold=true\x1b[0m",
+			expected: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?bold=true",
+			desc:     "Bold styled URL",
+		},
+		{
+			name:     "URL with underline ANSI",
+			output:   "\x1b[4mhttps://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?underline=true\x1b[0m",
+			expected: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?underline=true",
+			desc:     "Underlined URL",
+		},
+		{
+			name:     "URL with 256-color ANSI",
+			output:   "\x1b[38;5;33mhttps://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?color=256\x1b[0m",
+			expected: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?color=256",
+			desc:     "256-color foreground styled URL",
+		},
+		{
+			name:     "URL with RGB ANSI",
+			output:   "\x1b[38;2;100;150;200mhttps://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?rgb=true\x1b[0m",
+			expected: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?rgb=true",
+			desc:     "True-color (RGB) styled URL",
+		},
+		{
+			name:     "URL with cursor movement codes",
+			output:   "\x1b[2K\x1b[1Ghttps://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?cursor=move",
+			expected: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?cursor=move",
+			desc:     "URL after cursor movement/clear codes",
+		},
+		{
+			name:     "URL with multiple ANSI codes",
+			output:   "\x1b[1m\x1b[4m\x1b[36mhttps://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?multi=style\x1b[0m\x1b[0m\x1b[0m",
+			expected: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?multi=style",
+			desc:     "URL with stacked ANSI codes",
+		},
+		{
+			name:     "URL with ANSI in query params",
+			output:   "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?foo=\x1b[32mbar\x1b[0m&baz=qux",
+			expected: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?foo=bar&baz=qux",
+			desc:     "ANSI codes stripped before extraction - full URL preserved",
+		},
+		{
+			name:     "URL split by ANSI reset",
+			output:   "\x1b[36mhttps://claude.ai/oauth/authorize\x1b[0m?code=abc",
+			expected: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?code=abc",
+			desc:     "ANSI codes stripped first - full URL extracted intact",
+		},
+		{
+			name: "Complex terminal output with URL",
+			output: "\x1b[2J\x1b[H\x1b[?25l\x1b[1;1H\x1b[36m╭───────────────────────────────────────╮\x1b[0m\n" +
+				"\x1b[36m│\x1b[0m Please visit this URL:              \x1b[36m│\x1b[0m\n" +
+				"\x1b[36m│\x1b[0m https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?a=b \x1b[36m│\x1b[0m\n" +
+				"\x1b[36m╰───────────────────────────────────────╯\x1b[0m",
+			expected: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?a=b",
+			desc:     "URL in styled terminal box UI",
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tt := range tests {
+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			url := ExtractOAuthURL(tt.output)
+			if url != tt.expected {
+				t.Errorf("ExtractOAuthURL() = %q, want %q\nDescription: %s", url, tt.expected, tt.desc)
+			}
+			t.Logf("fixture=%s extraction_success=%v url_length=%d", tt.name, url == tt.expected, len(url))
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+func TestExtractOAuthURL_TrailingPunctuation(t *testing.T) {
+	tests := []struct {
+		name        string
+		output      string
+		expectedRaw string
+		desc        string
+	}{
+		{
+			name:        "trailing period",
+			output:      "Go to https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?x=1.",
+			expectedRaw: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?x=1.",
+			desc:        "Period after URL",
+		},
+		{
+			name:        "trailing comma",
+			output:      "URL is https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?x=1, then paste code",
+			expectedRaw: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?x=1,",
+			desc:        "Comma after URL",
+		},
+		{
+			name:        "trailing semicolon",
+			output:      "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?x=1; # comment",
+			expectedRaw: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?x=1;",
+			desc:        "Semicolon after URL",
+		},
+		{
+			name:        "trailing colon",
+			output:      "See https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?x=1: important!",
+			expectedRaw: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?x=1:",
+			desc:        "Colon after URL",
+		},
+		{
+			name:        "trailing exclamation",
+			output:      "Click https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?x=1!",
+			expectedRaw: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?x=1!",
+			desc:        "Exclamation after URL",
+		},
+		{
+			name:        "trailing paren",
+			output:      "(see https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?x=1)",
+			expectedRaw: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?x=1)",
+			desc:        "Closing paren after URL",
+		},
+		{
+			name:        "trailing bracket",
+			output:      "[https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?x=1]",
+			expectedRaw: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?x=1]",
+			desc:        "Closing bracket after URL",
+		},
+		{
+			name:        "trailing brace",
+			output:      "{url: https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?x=1}",
+			expectedRaw: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?x=1}",
+			desc:        "Closing brace after URL",
+		},
+		{
+			name:        "trailing angle bracket",
+			output:      "<https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?x=1>",
+			expectedRaw: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?x=1>",
+			desc:        "Closing angle bracket after URL",
+		},
+		{
+			name:        "multiple trailing punctuation",
+			output:      "URL: https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?x=1...",
+			expectedRaw: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?x=1...",
+			desc:        "Multiple periods after URL",
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tt := range tests {
+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			url := ExtractOAuthURL(tt.output)
+			if url != tt.expectedRaw {
+				t.Errorf("ExtractOAuthURL() = %q, want %q\nDescription: %s", url, tt.expectedRaw, tt.desc)
+			}
+			t.Logf("fixture=%s extraction_success=%v url_length=%d", tt.name, url == tt.expectedRaw, len(url))
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+func TestExtractOAuthURL_QueryParamPreservation(t *testing.T) {
+	tests := []struct {
+		name     string
+		output   string
+		expected string
+		desc     string
+	}{
+		{
+			name:     "standard OAuth params",
+			output:   "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?response_type=code&client_id=claude-code&redirect_uri=http%3A%2F%2Flocalhost%3A8080&scope=openid",
+			expected: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?response_type=code&client_id=claude-code&redirect_uri=http%3A%2F%2Flocalhost%3A8080&scope=openid",
+			desc:     "Standard OAuth 2.0 query params",
+		},
+		{
+			name:     "PKCE params",
+			output:   "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?code_challenge=E9Melhoa2OwvFrEMTJguCHaoeK1t8URWbuGJSstw-cM&code_challenge_method=S256",
+			expected: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?code_challenge=E9Melhoa2OwvFrEMTJguCHaoeK1t8URWbuGJSstw-cM&code_challenge_method=S256",
+			desc:     "PKCE code challenge params with Base64URL characters",
+		},
+		{
+			name:     "state param with special chars",
+			output:   "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?state=abc123_XYZ-789",
+			expected: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?state=abc123_XYZ-789",
+			desc:     "State param with underscores and hyphens",
+		},
+		{
+			name:     "nonce param",
+			output:   "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?nonce=f8a7d6c5b4e3a2b1",
+			expected: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?nonce=f8a7d6c5b4e3a2b1",
+			desc:     "Nonce param with hex string",
+		},
+		{
+			name:     "plus-encoded spaces",
+			output:   "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?scope=openid+profile+email",
+			expected: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?scope=openid+profile+email",
+			desc:     "Scope with plus-encoded spaces",
+		},
+		{
+			name:     "percent-encoded special chars",
+			output:   "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?redirect_uri=http%3A%2F%2Flocalhost%3A8080%2Fcallback%3Ffoo%3Dbar",
+			expected: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?redirect_uri=http%3A%2F%2Flocalhost%3A8080%2Fcallback%3Ffoo%3Dbar",
+			desc:     "Nested percent-encoded URL in redirect_uri",
+		},
+		{
+			name:     "empty param value",
+			output:   "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?empty=&foo=bar",
+			expected: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?empty=&foo=bar",
+			desc:     "Param with empty value",
+		},
+		{
+			name:     "param without value",
+			output:   "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?flag&foo=bar",
+			expected: "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?flag&foo=bar",
+			desc:     "Boolean flag param without equals sign",
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tt := range tests {
+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			url := ExtractOAuthURL(tt.output)
+			if url != tt.expected {
+				t.Errorf("ExtractOAuthURL() = %q, want %q\nDescription: %s", url, tt.expected, tt.desc)
+			}
+			t.Logf("fixture=%s extraction_success=%v url_length=%d", tt.name, url == tt.expected, len(url))
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+func TestDetectState_OAuthURLMetadata(t *testing.T) {
+	tests := []struct {
+		name        string
+		output      string
+		wantState   PaneState
+		wantURL     string
+		desc        string
+	}{
+		{
+			name:      "extracts URL to metadata",
+			output:    "Open https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?code=test123 in browser",
+			wantState: StateAwaitingURL,
+			wantURL:   "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?code=test123",
+			desc:      "URL should be stored in metadata",
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "URL with ANSI preserved in metadata",
+			output:    "\x1b[32mhttps://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?ansi=true\x1b[0m",
+			wantState: StateAwaitingURL,
+			wantURL:   "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?ansi=true\x1b[0m",
+			desc:      "URL extracted from original output - trailing ANSI captured (regex [^\\s]+ matches escape codes)",
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "paste prompt with URL",
+			output:    "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?x=1\nPaste code here if prompted >",
+			wantState: StateAwaitingURL,
+			wantURL:   "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?x=1",
+			desc:      "Paste prompt state extracts URL",
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tt := range tests {
+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			state, metadata := DetectState(tt.output)
+			if state != tt.wantState {
+				t.Errorf("DetectState() state = %v, want %v\nDescription: %s", state, tt.wantState, tt.desc)
+			}
+			if gotURL := metadata["oauth_url"]; gotURL != tt.wantURL {
+				t.Errorf("DetectState() metadata[oauth_url] = %q, want %q\nDescription: %s", gotURL, tt.wantURL, tt.desc)
+			}
+			t.Logf("fixture=%s state_correct=%v url_correct=%v url_length=%d",
+				tt.name, state == tt.wantState, metadata["oauth_url"] == tt.wantURL, len(metadata["oauth_url"]))
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+// TestOAuthURLPattern_Regex tests the regex pattern directly
+func TestOAuthURLPattern_Regex(t *testing.T) {
+	tests := []struct {
+		name       string
+		input      string
+		wantMatch  bool
+		wantFull   string
+		desc       string
+	}{
+		{
+			name:      "basic authorize URL",
+			input:     "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?code=abc",
+			wantMatch: true,
+			wantFull:  "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?code=abc",
+			desc:      "Simple URL matches",
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "URL without query string",
+			input:     "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize",
+			wantMatch: false,
+			wantFull:  "",
+			desc:      "URL without query requires at least ?",
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "URL with just question mark",
+			input:     "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?",
+			wantMatch: false,
+			wantFull:  "",
+			desc:      "URL with empty query string - regex requires at least one char after ?",
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "URL in text",
+			input:     "Please open https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?x=1 now",
+			wantMatch: true,
+			wantFull:  "https://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?x=1",
+			desc:      "URL extracted from surrounding text",
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "wrong domain",
+			input:     "https://example.com/oauth/authorize?code=abc",
+			wantMatch: false,
+			wantFull:  "",
+			desc:      "Non-claude domain should not match",
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "http instead of https",
+			input:     "http://claude.ai/oauth/authorize?code=abc",
+			wantMatch: false,
+			wantFull:  "",
+			desc:      "HTTP (non-secure) should not match",
+		},
+		{
+			name:      "wrong path",
+			input:     "https://claude.ai/api/authorize?code=abc",
+			wantMatch: false,
+			wantFull:  "",
+			desc:      "Wrong path should not match",
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tt := range tests {
+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			match := Patterns.OAuthURL.FindString(tt.input)
+			gotMatch := match != ""
+			if gotMatch != tt.wantMatch {
+				t.Errorf("OAuthURL.Match(%q) = %v, want %v\nDescription: %s", tt.input, gotMatch, tt.wantMatch, tt.desc)
+			}
+			if match != tt.wantFull {
+				t.Errorf("OAuthURL.FindString(%q) = %q, want %q", tt.input, match, tt.wantFull)
+			}
+			t.Logf("fixture=%s match=%v extracted_length=%d", tt.name, gotMatch, len(match))
+		})
+	}
+}
